big_picture: Today's top Indian policy news revolves around Trade, Energy, and Politics. Mamata Banerjee has slammed the BJP and Election Commission over officer transfers before assembly polls, calling them "anti-women" and "anti-Bengal." Meanwhile, the BJP has gained an upper hand in Odisha Rajya Sabha polls amid cross-voting drama. The Indian Navy has taken steps to secure Indian vessels in the Hormuz Strait, with the Indian LPG carrier Shivalik reaching Mundra Port in Gujarat, carrying 40,000 metric tonnes of liquefied petroleum gas.

Legal.summary:
The Supreme Court plans to issue guidelines to High Courts to avoid delays in pronouncing judgments, with suggestions including a 3-month deadline and displaying pending judgments on HC websites. 

The Kerala High Court has reaffirmed that disputes concerning elections to Bar Associations are matters of internal administration and not maintainable through writ petitions.
Legal.top_article_title: Supreme Court To Issue Guidelines To High Courts To Avoid Delay In Pronouncing Judgments; Amicus Gives Suggestions

Foreign.summary:
India has criticized Pakistan over air-bombings against Afghanistan at the United Nations General Assembly. 

India is facing a diplomatic challenge between Iran and the US, which could impact its economy, particularly during the BRICS forum it is chairing this year. 

The Reserve Bank of Australia has increased its cash rate in response to a global energy shock.
Foreign.top_article_title: India slams Pakistan over air-bombings against Afghanistan in UNGA

Energy.summary:
The Indian government has announced changes to the Captive Power Policy through the Electricity (Amendment) Rules, 2026. The changes aim to modify Rule 3 of the Electricity Rules, which govern captive generation.

India is facing an LPG shortage due to the Strait of Hormuz blockade amid the war between the US, Israel, and Iran in West Asia. A second LPG tanker, Nanda Devi, has arrived at Kandla Port, carrying 46,000 metric tonnes of LPG to help alleviate the crisis.
Energy.top_article_title: Daily News Wrap-Up: Government Announces Changes to Captive Power Policy

Finance.summary:
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) held a 7-day Variable Rate Repo (VRR) auction on March 17, 2026, allotting the entire notified amount of ₹1,50,000 crore at a cut-off rate. 

The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released the Money Market Operations as of March 16, 2026, showing the weighted average rate range for various segments. 

The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has issued a consultation paper on revised nomination norms for demat accounts and mutual funds, seeking public suggestions to enhance the nomination facilities.
Finance.top_article_title: Result of the 7-day Variable Rate Repo (VRR) auction held on March 17, 2026

Agriculture.summary:
The Lok Sabha revoked the suspension of eight Opposition MPs and the Rajya Sabha discussed the working of the Rural Development Ministry during the Parliament Budget Session. 

The Ministry of Fertilizers launched a ₹6,350-crore expansion project, including a Phosphoric Acid Plant and a Sulphuric Acid Plant at Cochin Division, to boost fertilizer self-reliance. 

The GSP Crop Science IPO received a 61% subscription on its second day, with a 47% subscription on the first day, and aims to raise ₹400 crore.
Agriculture.top_article_title: Parliament Budget Session LIVE: Lok Sabha revokes suspension of eight Opposition MPs; Rajya Sabha discusses working of the Rural Development Ministry

Health.summary:
The Rajasthan government is planning to vaccinate over two crore cattle heads against foot-and-mouth disease as part of its seventh phase of vaccination. 

The Indian National Congress has condemned the Pakistani strike on an Afghanistan hospital, calling it "barbarism" and urging the world to reject such actions.
Health.top_article_title: Rajasthan to vaccinate 2 cr cattle heads against foot-and-mouth disease

Politics.summary:
Dilip Ghosh, a BJP leader, announced his intention to contest the upcoming West Bengal polls from the Kharagpur Sadar constituency, stating that the BJP aims to gain more votes than in the last Assembly elections.

West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee criticized the Election Commission and the BJP, labeling their sudden transfer of top state officials as an "anti-Bengal and anti-women" move ahead of the assembly polls.

The Indian Journal of Law and Legal Research is issuing a call for papers for its upcoming Volume 8, Issue 1, and is available in various academic libraries worldwide, including those in the European Parliament and top universities like Harvard and Stanford.
Politics.top_article_title: West Bengal polls 2026: Dilip Ghosh to contest from Kharagpur Sadar

Trade.summary:
Opposition MPs in Rajya Sabha have requested a discussion on the working of either the Commerce Ministry or the External Affairs Ministry. 

India's trade deal with the US will be signed once America re-establishes global tariff rates, according to Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal.
Trade.top_article_title: Opposition MPs demand discussion on either Commerce Ministry or MEA in Rajya Sabha

Defence.summary: India has strongly condemned a Pakistan airstrike on a Kabul rehabilitation hospital, calling it a "cowardly" and "unconscionable" act that Pakistan is attempting to portray as a military operation. India has urged the international community to hold the perpetrators accountable and ensure that Pakistan stops targeting civilians.
Defence.top_article_title: ‘Pakistan dressing up massacre as military op’: India slams ‘barbaric’ airstrike on Kabul rehabilitation hospital

Civil Liberties.summary: The Calcutta High Court's order for an NIA probe into Beldanga violence has been upheld by the Supreme Court, which declined to intervene in the matter. The Congress party has also expressed its views on the suspension of 8 Lok Sabha MPs, stating that the 'Lakshman Rekha' must include the dignity of the Leader of Opposition.
Civil Liberties.top_article_title: Suspension of 8 Lok Sabha MPs revoked, Congress says: ‘Lakshman Rekha must include dignity of LoP’

Governance.summary:
A 28-year-old man in Delhi allegedly shot himself while his cousin was recording a video of him loading a pistol, and the video has gone viral. 

The National Democratic Alliance (NDA) has fielded a father-son duo in two Christian-dominated constituencies in Kerala ahead of the April 9 Assembly polls. 

The Delhi High Court has issued summons to Republic TV's Arnab Goswami on a Congress plea over a defamation case related to his claim that the party has an office in Turkey.
Governance.top_article_title: Cousin Records As Man Shoots Self While Loading Pistol in Delhi

Infrastructure.summary:
A foot overbridge collapsed in Delhi's Roop Nagar area, resulting in the death of a 50-year-old woman who fell into a nearby 'nala'. The incident occurred on Tuesday morning. 

The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has attached assets worth ₹23.35 crore in a compensation scam case related to the Raipur-Visakhapatnam highway.
Infrastructure.top_article_title: Iron Bridge Collapses in Delhi's Roop Nagar, Woman Dead

Technology.summary:
The Madras High Court has deferred its orders on writ petitions challenging tender conditions for live webcasting in the Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026 until March 17. 

Banks and their customers are facing challenges with Know Your Customer (KYC) compliance, causing strain on staff and account holders. 

A global memory chip shortage is predicted to continue until 2030 by Chey Tae-won, SK Group chairman, due to supply constraints by major companies such as SK Hynix, Samsung, and Micron.
Technology.top_article_title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Madras High Court defers orders in cases challenging tender conditions for live webcasting

Welfare.summary: The Supreme Court of India has allowed its first passive euthanasia case, granting permission to withdraw life support from Harish Rana, a resident of Ghaziabad who has been in a coma since suffering a head injury. The court clarified the best interest test in this decision, allowing for the withdrawal of life-sustaining treatment.
Welfare.top_article_title: Supreme Court Allows First Passive Euthanasia, Grants Permission to Withdraw Life Support

Environment.summary:
A study suggests that air pollution in Delhi may harbor drug-resistant bacteria, posing health risks to millions of residents. 

India is embracing coffee as a potential solution to climate change, with efforts to promote sustainable coffee production. 

Efforts are being made to protect and revive India's rivers, as part of the International Day of Action for Rivers 2026, which pays tribute to environmental leader R. Nallakannu of Tamil Nadu.
Environment.top_article_title: Study suggests air pollution may harbour drug-resistant bacteria

Labour.summary:
The State of Working India 2026 report highlights India's growing young workforce, increasing tertiary enrolment, and reduced disparities, but warns of high graduate unemployment and the need for urgent job creation. 

Infosys is expanding its Mohali Development Center, adding a 3.5 lakh sq ft built-up area that will seat about 3,000 employees, strengthening its capabilities. 

Punjab's Economic Survey 2025-26 notes that a combination of "pull" and "push" factors, including economic stagnation and job mismatch, is behind the distress migration of people from the state.
Labour.top_article_title: India's young workforce is growing and getting more educated: State of Working India 2026 report

Education.summary: LTM has partnered with IIT-Kharagpur to create training programs for employees, focusing on artificial intelligence and related technologies. The collaboration aims to upskill the workforce in these areas.
Education.top_article_title: LTM ties up with IIT-Kharagpur to upskill workforce in artificial intelligence

Urban.summary: Hard Rock International terminated franchise agreements with JSM Corporation Pvt Ltd, resulting in the closure of Hard Rock Cafes and Rock Shops across 10 locations in India. The move marks the end of the partnership between the two entities.
Urban.top_article_title: Hard Rock Cafe terminates agreement with JSM Corp, shuts outlets across 10 locations in India

articles.title: Co-Owner's Undertaking To Accept “Whatever Amount” On Future Sale Cannot Bar Statutory Right To Partition: Kerala High Court
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court has held that an indefinite undertaking by a co-owner agreeing to accept “whatever amount” another party may pay on a future sale of property cannot bar a statutory right to seek partition. The Division Bench of Justice Sathish Ninan and Justice P. Krishna Kumar delivered the judgment in a regular first appeal, affirming a preliminary decree for partition passed by the Principal Sub Court, Palakkad. The case arose from a partition suit filed by a sister against her brother

The decision provides clarity on compliance requirements for trusts managing accumulated funds.

articles.title: Can a Public Servant Escape Bribery Conviction Because the Co-Accused Was Acquitted? Supreme Court Clarifies Law
articles.summary: The Supreme Court clarified that a public servant convicted of bribery cannot automatically escape punishment just because a co-accused was acquitted. The ruling emphasizes that the failure to prove conspiracy against one accused does not invalidate the conviction of another in bribery cases. This decision reinforces the legal framework around corruption prosecutions in India.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court explains that failure to prove conspiracy against a co-accused does not entitle a convicted public servant to acquittal in bribery cases.

The company is involved in power generation and distribution in India.

articles.title: CESTAT Allows Cenvat Credit as Steel Items Used for Plant Maintenance Qualify as Inputs
articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal (CESTAT) ruled that steel items used for maintaining and repairing manufacturing machinery qualify as 'inputs' under Cenvat credit rules. This means businesses can claim tax credits for such purchases. The tribunal set aside demands for credit reversal and penalties, clarifying that plant maintenance materials are eligible for input tax credits. The decision impacts manufacturers' tax compliance and financial planning.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal held that steel items used for maintenance and repair of manufacturing machinery fall within the scope of inputs used in relation to manufacture. The demand for reversal of Cenvat credit and penalty was set aside. The post CESTAT Allows Cenvat Credit as Steel Items Used for Plant Maintenance Qualify as Inputs appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Political crossovers gather pace in Kerala as Assembly elections near

Support measures for exporters on anvil to deal with West Asia crisis
articles.summary: The commerce ministry is set to announce support measures for Indian exporters facing disruptions due to the West Asia crisis. These measures, potentially including insurance assistance, aim to mitigate the impact of increased freight costs and premiums. The government is actively monitoring the situation and collaborating with various departments to aid the trading community.

articles.title: 2026 LiveLaw (SC) 246 |  THE STATE OF HIMACHAL PRADESH VERSUS SURAT SINGH
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that giving an accused the option to be searched before a police officer violates Section 50 of the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) Act. The case involved the State of Himachal Pradesh versus Surat Singh. This ruling impacts how police conduct searches under the NDPS Act, which deals with drug-related offenses. The decision underscores strict procedural compliance in drug law enforcement.

articles.title: 2026 LiveLaw (SC) 245  |  THE MANAGING DIRECTOR, KSRTC VERSUS P. CHANDRAMOULI & ORS.
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that employer-provided group insurance benefits cannot be deducted from compensation awarded in motor accident claims. The case involved the Managing Director of KSRTC (Kerala State Road Transport Corporation) and P. Chandramouli & others. The ruling clarifies legal standards for compensation in motor accident cases, ensuring victims receive full benefits without offsetting employer insurance payouts.

articles.title: Motor Accident Claim: No Deduction Of Employer-Provided Group Insurance Benefits From Compensation, Reiterates Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India reiterated that employer-provided group insurance benefits cannot be deducted from motor accident compensation. The Court clarified that contractual or social security benefits are not subject to such deductions. This ruling impacts how compensation is calculated in motor accident claims across the country.
articles.raw_summary: Contractual or social security benefits cannot be deducted from the compensation, the Court stated.

articles.title: Phantom Precedents: The Rise Of AI-Generated Case Law In Indian Courts
articles.summary: The legitimacy of common law adjudication depends on the authenticity of precedent. If fabricated authorities enter judicial reasoning, the integrity of the doctrine of stare decisis itself is compromised. Recently, however, a disruptive and highly concerning trend has emerged across global jurisdictions, the submission of phantom case law. Judicial officers reviewing written submissions are increasingly discovering perfectly formatted citations for judgments that simply do not exist. The culpri

articles.title: Boney Kapoor And Daughters Move Madras High Court To Reject Plaint Claiming Sridevi's Property
articles.summary: Film Producer Boney Kapoor and his daughters, Janhvi and Kushi Kapoor, have approached the Madras High Court against an order of the Additional District Judge, Chengalpattu, refusing to reject a plaint filed in connection with the late actor Sridevi's property near the East Coast Road. When the plea came up before Justice TV Thamilselvi on Monday (16 March), the court decided to take up the matter for final disposal on March 26, 2026 and extended the interim order staying the trial in the case.

articles.title: Easier FDI norms notified for border country investors
articles.summary: India has updated its foreign direct investment policy. Investments from countries sharing a land border now face stricter government scrutiny. Overseas companies with up to 10% Chinese shareholding can invest via the automatic route, subject to limits. However, entities registered in China or other bordering nations require government approval. This change aims to regulate investments from neighboring countries.

articles.title: What led to PhonePe delaying its IPO? Beyond war, valuation a major concern
articles.summary: PhonePe, a Walmart-owned digital payments firm valued at $15 billion, has delayed its planned $1.3 billion IPO due to volatile markets, valuation concerns, and geopolitical tensions. Investor interest was lower than expected at around $7 billion. The delay is also attributed to competition with Paytm and regulatory challenges that impact profitability and listing timing.

The Walmart-owned firm, valued at nearly $15 billion, saw investor interest closer to $7 billion. Competition with Paytm and regulatory headwinds also affected its profitability outlook and listing timing.

articles.title: PhonePe hits pause on IPO as Iran war roils primary market sentiment
articles.summary: Digital payments giant PhonePe has postponed its planned public listing in India. Citing global geopolitical conflicts and market instability, CEO Sameer Nigam expressed hope for a swift return to peace. The company, boasting over 65 crore users and a vast merchant network, remains committed to an Indian IPO once markets stabilize.

articles.summary: Databricks is ramping up hiring and investment in India, expanding its Bengaluru R D centre as enterprise demand grows. articles.title: India growth outlook dims as oil tops $100, gas crisis deepens
articles.summary: A distant war is impacting India's economy. Soaring oil prices and gas shortages are affecting homes and businesses. This energy crisis threatens economic growth and raises inflation concerns. Industries are halting operations. The situation is creating strain for everyday citizens and impacting employment. The external sector faces significant risks as well.

articles.title: Biz chamber seeks RBI relief for exporters hit by West Asia disruptions
articles.summary: Indian exporters are facing significant challenges due to global shipping route disruptions. The Bharat Chamber of Commerce has requested the Reserve Bank of India to provide crucial banking support. This includes enhanced working capital, flexible credit, and extended repayment periods. The aim is to safeguard exporters from financial strain and ensure continued trade flow amidst these global uncertainties.

articles.title: India’s wholesale inflation rises to 11-month high of 2.13% in February
articles.summary: India's wholesale price inflation reached an 11-month high of 2.13% in February, driven by increased costs of primary goods like food and non-food articles. Economists anticipate further acceleration due to rising input costs and ongoing geopolitical tensions, with manufacturing products also showing a slight increase.

articles.summary: India's trade deficit widened significantly in February, reaching $27 billion. This increase was driven by a substantial rise in imports, especially gold and silver. Exports experienced a minor decrease. The government plans to introduce measures to support exporters facing logistical challenges. The trade gap with China also saw an increase during the fiscal year.

articles.summary: India-US trade deal signing is contingent on a new tariff framework, with discussions ongoing regarding non-tariff barriers and Section 232 tariffs. articles.title: Revamped Stand-Up India scheme soon: Nirmala Sitharaman
articles.summary: Replying to a supplementary question in the Lok Sabha, Sitharaman said the modified programme would incorporate lessons from the earlier scheme, which was discontinued in April 2025 after nine years. "With studies done by Niti Aayog and others, this scheme is being redrafted and soon the Cabinet will be given a proposal with which we will launch a new scheme to continue with Stand-Up India, which benefitted SC/ST and women all over the country," she said.

articles.summary: India's horticulture production is set to remain steady in 2025-26. Vegetable output is expected to rise, driven by onions and potatoes. Fruit production will see a slight increase. articles.title: Govt approves additional 87,587 tonnes sugar export quota for 2025-26
articles.summary: In a significant move, India's food ministry has greenlit an additional quota of 87,587 tonnes of sugar exports for the upcoming 2025-26 season, responding to demands from sugar mills. These mills are required to complete the exports by June 30, 2026, with those meeting a threshold of 70 percent allowed to ship the remaining sugar by September 30, 2026.

articles.title: DPIIT notifies changes in FDI norms for China, other land border sharing countries
articles.summary: India has taken a bold step toward enhancing its investment landscape by relaxing restrictions on foreign investments. articles.summary: India has initiated negotiations for trade agreements with the Philippines and the Maldives, signing terms of reference for a preferential trade agreement (PTA) with the former and a free trade agreement (FTA) with the latter. These moves come as India reviews its existing trade pacts with ASEAN and Korea, and pursues new agreements with several other nations and blocs.

articles.title: Shipping wars stitch higher costs into India’s apparel exports
articles.summary: India's apparel exporters are facing increased costs due to the West Asian conflict, with shipping routes extended and war surcharges adding ₹12 to ₹55 per garment. This disruption, including diversions around the Cape of Good Hope, delays deliveries by 10-15 days and impacts a key market for Indian ready-made garments.

articles.title: Unemployment rate eases to 4.9% in February
articles.summary: India's unemployment rate dipped to 4.9% in February, a slight improvement from January's 5%, largely due to a significant decrease in the female jobless rate. Urban unemployment also saw a decline, while rural rates remained stable. Youth unemployment, however, experienced a marginal increase.

Urban unemployment also saw a slight decrease, while rural unemployment remained steady. The overall Labour Force Participation Rate held firm, though female participation saw a marginal increase, particularly in rural areas.

articles.title: CCI plans to check abuse in AI space
articles.summary: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) has issued a guidance note urging stakeholders such as boards, management teams, and decision-makers to conduct self-audits of their AI systems. The goal is to ensure that AI applications do not have hidden anti-competitive outcomes during development, deployment, and monitoring. This move aims to proactively address potential competition issues in the rapidly evolving AI space in India.

articles.title: India needs outcome-driven urban policy, says EAC-PM paper
articles.summary: The paper titled ‘India’s Hidden Urbanisation and its Policy Implications’ written by EAC-PM member Shamika Ravi and its consultants Manuj Joshi and Apurv Kumar Mishra suggests disbursement of funds under Union schemes over the next five years to be linked to needs on the ground along with financing, governance and land reforms in the sector.

While the broader consumption outlook remains resilient, inflation risks are increasing.

articles.summary: Indian basmati rice exports are projected to stay stable this fiscal and next. Growth of up to two percent is anticipated over last year's volume. These changes indicate growing cost pressures across key economic sectors.

This strategy helps manage liquidity and keep borrowing costs low for banks.

articles.title: Rajya Sabha Election 2026: What is cross-voting
articles.summary: Cross-voting in Rajya Sabha elections refers to when a Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) votes for a candidate from a different party, contrary to their own party's directive or candidate preferences.
articles.raw_summary: Cross-voting in Rajya Sabha elections refers to when a Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) votes for a candidate from a different party, contrary to their own party's directive or candidate preferences.

articles.title: Assembly elections 2026: EC imposes Model Code of Conduct; what's not allowed in poll-bound states
articles.summary: The Election Commission has imposed the Model Code of Conduct for the upcoming assembly elections in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Assam, and Puducherry. This restricts certain activities in these poll-bound states to ensure fair elections. The code is a regulatory measure that impacts political campaigns and governance during the election period.
articles.raw_summary: Assembly elections will be held in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala and Assam, along with the Union Territory of Puducherry.

articles.title: BJP eyes gains in polls to five assemblies; West Bengal main challenge

articles.title: Polls to five assembles crucial for Left as it eyes Kerala hat-trick, Bengal revival
articles.summary: The upcoming elections in five states are crucial for Left parties, especially in Kerala, where the LDF aims for a third consecutive term. CPI(M) General Secretary MA Baby has emphasized their preparedness for these polls. The outcome could influence the political landscape and policy direction in these states.
articles.raw_summary: The poll schedule for five assemblies is crucial for Left parties. CPI(M) General Secretary MA Baby emphasized their readiness, especially in Kerala, where the LDF seeks a third term.

articles.title: From tiger reserves to hill treks: ECI highlights India’s most unique polling stations
articles.summary: The Election Commission highlighted some of India’s most unique polling stations, located in remote forests, hill ranges and tribal regions across multiple states. Polling teams often travel long distances by ferry, tractor or on foot to ensure every voter can cast their ballot. We take a look at some.

articles.title: Churning within TVK; to be lone ''guiding light'' of Tamil Nadu or be part of NDA
articles.summary: TVK party leaders are reportedly discussing a potential alliance with the NDA, despite public denials. Internal discussions aim to leverage the charisma of actor Vijay and the strength of seasoned parties to create electoral synergy. The outcome could impact Tamil Nadu's political landscape ahead of elections.

articles.title: LPG cargo vessel Shivalik reaches Mundra with 46,000 tonnes of fuel amid Hormuz tensions
articles.summary: The cargo vessel Shivalik arrived at Mundra Port in Gujarat with 46,000 metric tonnes of LPG. Of this, 20,000 MT will be unloaded at Mundra and 26,000 MT at Mangalore. Another vessel, Nanda Devi, is expected to dock on Tuesday. The shipment occurs amid heightened tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global oil and gas trade.

This development could impact India's trade flows and energy imports, particularly crude oil, which often transits through the region.
